findings.description: No candidate rules were produced for this blob at all despite five distinct tint/transparency specifications and a closed-enumeration compliance statement present in the source. This is a total extraction miss requiring the whole cluster to be built from scratch.

rules.scope: campaign headline D/M (Gold or Brand Blue colorway)

findings.description: u_0005 is a normative closed-enumeration compliance statement ('No other ad-hoc tints — use palette values or these specified tints only') that establishes hard governance (prohibition + required-fallback) over all tint rules in this cluster. It must be extracted as its own governed rule with hardness=hard, polarity=prohibition, and an explicit relation binding it to the specific tint rules (u_0001–u_0004) it governs; currently no such rule or relation exists.

payload.statement: No ad-hoc tints permitted; only palette values or the specified tint/transparency values may be used.

findings.description: u_0004 specifies 'transparency' not 'tint' — a distinct visual property from the tint percentages in u_0001-u_0003. If merged under a single generic 'tint rule' entity, the transparency vs tint distinction (scope/attribute type) would be lost; should be tagged with attribute=transparency rather than tint to avoid scope conflation.

u_0001 rule scope is compound (applies only to campaign headlines 'D' and 'M', restricted to Gold or Brand Blue colorway) and uses an approximate qualifier ('≈40%'). Any extracted rule must preserve both the restrictive scope and the approximate/soft-value polarity rather than treating it as an exact hard numeric equal to the other 70%/30% exact tint rules.
